_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Maintain and optimize existing BI assets, including performance tuning, data model updates, and deprecation of unused content.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI AB domain. Task AB-T-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-008 (DQ, cosine: 0.448), AG-T-002 (AG, cosine: 0.421).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Minimal similarity (1.6/10). Near-zero cross-encoder score indicates the concepts share little semantic content despite being mapped as related.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: Lower token-level alignment (F1=0.849). The concepts share limited contextual embedding similarity at the token level, consistent with cross-domain vocabulary divergence.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Contradiction-dominant (99.8%). The model detects scope divergence — the concepts may use similar vocabulary in opposing contexts. This warrants review but may reflect domain-specific usage patterns rather than true semantic conflict.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder (0.459) exceeds STS (0.162). The independent embeddings find more similarity than cross-attention, suggesting surface-level vocabulary overlap exceeds the deeper semantic relationship.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (5.6%). Limited shared vocabulary — the relationship is primarily semantic rather than terminological.
